articles.title: Newlywed Wife of U.S. Soldier Released From Immigration Detention
articles.summary: The couple had gone to the husband’s Army base to complete paperwork so they could move in together. But within hours that plan derailed, and New York Times reporting about the case quickly spread.

articles.title: ‘Definitely a Sham’: As Tariffs Climb, Trade Fraud and Accounting Tricks Proliferate
articles.summary: U.S. imports from China have shrunk drastically. But billions of dollars of the change appear to be the result of accounting gimmicks and outright fraud.

articles.title: Taiwan's opposition leader arrives in China for a 'Journey of Peace'
articles.summary: The visit takes place ahead of President Trump's own summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next month, where Taiwan is expected to be a top agenda item.

articles.title: Iranians Voice Shock and Defiance in Face of Trump’s Looming Deadline
articles.summary: President Trump has threatened devastating attacks if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Some Iranians questioned what had happened to American values.

articles.title: Shelly Kittleson, American Journalist Abducted in Iraq, Is Freed
articles.summary: The journalist, Shelly Kittleson, was abducted by a militia allied with Iran and held for a week. Iraqi officials say she was freed in exchange for the release of militia members.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Spread ‘Moon Joy’ to the Public
articles.summary: While science can seem colorless and plain, NASA’s lunar crew members have brought expressiveness and emotion about their journey to mission control and the public.

articles.title: Trump Threatens Broad Attacks in Iran. How Is Illegal Conduct in War Defined?
articles.summary: It is illegal for any military to target civilians, as President Trump has suggested he would in threats against Iran. But the U.S. has sought significant leeway in defining a civilian target.

articles.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife
articles.summary: The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in

articles.title: Finasteride for Male Baldness is Rewriting the Rules of Male Beauty
articles.summary: A pill to cure baldness is changing the way men age — and how they see themselves.

articles.title: Despite High Oil Prices and Volatile Stocks, Companies See Openings for Deals
articles.summary: Bankers say companies are braving higher oil prices and whipsawing stock prices to seize on the willingness of federal antitrust enforcers to approve mergers.

articles.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? Americans weigh options for saving gas
articles.summary: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.

articles.title: Photos: NASA releases first images from moon flyby
articles.summary: During the mission's loop around the moon, the crew took geological observations of places of interest on the lunar surface using their own eyes and snapping thousands of photos of the surface.

articles.title: Australia charges ex‑soldier with 5 war‑crime murders in Afghanistan
articles.summary: Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.

articles.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
articles.summary: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

articles.title: Trump warns 'a whole civilization will die tonight' if Iran fails to meet his deadline
articles.summary: With the clock ticking, President Trump radically expanded his threat to the entire Iranian nation if its leaders fail to do a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday night, U.S. Eastern time.

articles.title: How Bill Phillips used flowing water to model the economy
articles.summary: Bill Phillips was an outsider to economics, but he used a machine and a chart to change the way we think about the government's role in a capitalist economy.

articles.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
articles.summary: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

articles.title: Vance visits Hungary to bolster support for prime minister ahead of election
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance is in Budapest to join an election rally for incumbent Prime Minister Viktor Orbán ahead of Hungary's election.

articles.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
articles.summary: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.

articles.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
articles.summary: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
